stem: In how many ways can a committee of 2 people be chosen from a group of 5 people?
In how many ways can a committee of 3 people be chosen from a group of 6 people?
Compare the two quantities.
quantityA: The number of ways to choose 2 people from a group of 5 (order doesn't matter)
quantityB: The number of ways to arrange 2 people from a group of 5 in a line (order matters)
solutionStandard: Combinations: \(C(5,2)=10\). Permutations: \(P(5,2)=20\). Since order-matters counting is always at least as large, Quantity B is greater.

stem: A standard deck has 52 cards. In how many ways can a hand of 2 cards be chosen (order doesn't matter)?
A pizza shop offers 6 toppings. How many different 3-topping pizzas can be made (no repeated toppings, order doesn't matter)?
A group has 4 women and 5 men. In how many ways can a team of 2 women and 2 men be chosen?
solutionStandard: Choose women: \(C(4,2)=6\). Choose men: \(C(5,2)=10\). Total: \(6 \times 10 = 60\).

stem: Which of the following scenarios are combination problems (order doesn't matter)? Select all that apply.

options.text: Choosing 3 books to bring on a trip from a shelf of 10
Awarding gold, silver, and bronze medals to 3 of 10 racers
Selecting a 5-person jury from a pool of 20
Assigning students to numbered seats in a row

solutionStandard: (A) and (C) are unordered selections — a combination. (B) assigns distinct roles (gold/silver/bronze) to specific people, so order/position matters — a permutation. (D) assigns distinct seat numbers, so it's also a permutation.

stem: In how many ways can a committee of at least 1 person be chosen from a group of 3 people?
A committee of 4 is chosen from 5 women and 4 men. In how many ways can the committee include exactly 2 women and 2 men?
solutionStandard: Choose 2 of 5 women: \(C(5,2)=10\). Choose 2 of 4 men: \(C(4,2)=6\). Total: \(10 \times 6 = 60\).

stem: How many diagonals does a hexagon (6 sides) have?
solutionStandard: The number of segments connecting any 2 of the 6 vertices is \(C(6,2)=15\). Subtract the 6 sides themselves (which connect adjacent vertices, not diagonals): \(15-6=9\).

stem: In how many ways can a committee of {{k}} people be chosen from a group of {{n}} people?
In how many ways can a committee of 2 people be chosen from a group of 7 people?
Compare the two quantities.
quantityA: The number of ways to choose {{k}} people from a group of {{n}} (order doesn't matter)
quantityB: The number of ways to arrange {{k}} people from a group of {{n}} in a line (order matters)
solutionStandard: Permutations count every ordering of the same combination separately (there are \(k!\) orderings per combination, and \(k! \ge 2\) whenever \(k \ge 2\)), so \(P(n,k) = C(n,k) \times k!\) is always greater than \(C(n,k)\). Quantity B is greater.

stem: A collection has {{pool}} distinct items. In how many ways can {{k}} of them be chosen (order doesn't matter)?
solutionStandard: \(C({{pool}},{{k}})\): multiply {{k}} descending values starting from {{pool}}, then divide by {{k}}!.

stem: A sandwich shop offers 7 ingredients. How many different 4-ingredient sandwiches can be made (no repeated ingredients, order doesn't matter)?
A group has {{w}} women and {{m}} men. In how many ways can a team of 2 women and 2 men be chosen?
answerFormula: (function(){function C(n,k){let r=1;for(let i=0;i<k;i++)r*=(n-i);for(let i=1;i<=k;i++)r/=i;return Math.round(r);} return C(w,2)*C(m,2);})()
solutionStandard: Choose 2 women: \(C({{w}},2)\). Choose 2 men: \(C({{m}},2)\). Multiply the two counts together.

stem: Which of the following scenarios are permutation problems (order matters)? Select all that apply.

options.text: Choosing 4 toppings for a pizza from 8 options
Ranking the top 3 finishers out of 8 racers
Selecting 2 equal-role co-captains from a team of 12
Assigning a unique locker number to each of 5 students

solutionStandard: (B) assigns distinct ranks (1st, 2nd, 3rd) to specific racers, so order matters — a permutation. (D) assigns a distinct locker number to each student, also a permutation. (A) and (C) are unordered selections — combinations, not permutations.

stem: In how many ways can a committee of at least 2 people be chosen from a group of 4 people?
A committee of 4 is chosen from {{w}} women and {{m}} men. In how many ways can the committee include exactly 2 women and 2 men?
answerFormula: (function(){function C(n,k){let r=1;for(let i=0;i<k;i++)r*=(n-i);for(let i=1;i<=k;i++)r/=i;return Math.round(r);} return C(w,2)*C(m,2);})()
solutionStandard: Choose 2 of {{w}} women: \(C({{w}},2)\). Choose 2 of {{m}} men: \(C({{m}},2)\). Multiply the two counts together.

stem: How many diagonals does a polygon with {{n}} sides have?
solutionStandard: The number of segments connecting any 2 of the {{n}} vertices is \(C({{n}},2)\). Subtracting the {{n}} sides (which connect adjacent vertices, not diagonals) simplifies to \(\dfrac{n(n-3)}{2} = {{n*(n-3)/2}}\).
